element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Speed statistic becomes relevant as player gains characters
Progressive unlocking as party expands to 14 playable characters
Party division in dungeons requiring strategic coordination
Multi-party splits required for later dungeons
Airship unlocked mid-to-late game for expanded map access
Activated by visiting specific towns at story milestones
Thematic escalation involving rebellion against military dictatorship and chemical weapons

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe towns with shops and NPCs
typical_tension_triggers: Random enemy encounters on overworld and field maps
Dungeon progression with puzzles and mazes
Boss encounters tied to story beats
Multi-party split dungeon segments requiring coordination
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Oscillating between exploration-driven tension (random encounters) and relief (towns/menus), with escalating emotional intensity through darker story themes in mid-to-late game

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Treasure chests in dungeons contain rare items not available in stores, signaling reward for exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to Active Time Battle system with safe encounters; low-threat tutorial design
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty with dungeon puzzles, multi-party splits, and expanding party composition (14 possible characters); random encounter scaling
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty with apocalyptic story confrontations, violent narrative events, and complex dungeon mechanics requiring strategic coordination
notable_spikes: Narrative turning point: revelation of military dictatorship threat
Introduction of chemical weapons/destructive elements in plot
Multi-party split dungeons requiring simultaneous progression

notable_level_patterns.description: Story development follows progression through towns (NPC interaction, shops, exposition) → dungeon exploration (puzzles, treasures, combat) → overworld navigation (chocobo/airship travel, random encounters)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic progression structure repeated across game

notable_level_patterns.description: Later dungeons require dividing the 14-character roster into multiple parties that must work together and progress simultaneously, creating coordination challenges
Side-quests and certain towns activate only when visited at specific story milestones, gating optional content behind narrative progression

notes: Data Crystal and TCRF sources provided primarily ROM/technical metadata and navigation content respectively, with no substantive design documentation. Wikipedia article provided gameplay mechanics, narrative structure, and design patterns. T1 tier data relies on qualitative descriptors from gameplay description; no quantitative level sequencing data found in sources.
